message.content:
Update documentation in the BeFirst_Docs repository at S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Docs for a new feature.

**New Feature: Areas Relation Management (PLEX Operations Mapping)**

A new management page was added to the Manage area of the BeFirst Portal. It allows mapping BeFirst internal areas (from PART_OPERATION_AREAS table) to PLEX ERP operations (from PART_OPERATION table) via the PART_OPERATION_AREAS_RELATION junction table.

**What to document:**

1. Read `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Docs\docs\06_WORKFLOWS.md` and find the Manage area section. Add a note about the new "Areas Relation" management page. It provides:
   - Full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) for mapping BeFirst areas to PLEX operations
   - Fields: Area (dropdown), Side (A/B), PLEX Operation (dropdown from PART_OPERATION table), Plex Operation Code, Operation Key, Description, Flag, PCN (default 278344), Facility
   - Accessible via Manage > Stations > Areas Relation menu
   - Stored in PART_OPERATION_AREAS_RELATION table in MES database

2. Read `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Docs\docs\12_CHANGELOG.md` and add a new entry under the existing 2026-02-24 section (don't create a duplicate date header):
   - FEAT: Add Areas Relation management page (Manage area) for mapping BeFirst areas to PLEX ERP operations
   - Enables configuration of area-to-operation mapping with full CRUD support

3. Read `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Docs\docs\04_DATABASE.md` and add the PART_OPERATION_AREAS_RELATION table schema if not already there:
   - Columns: AutoID (INT, IDENTITY), Plex_Operation_Code (VARCHAR(50)), AreaID (INT, NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0), Side (VARCHAR(3)), Flag (BIT, DEFAULT 0), PCN (INT, DEFAULT 278344), Operation_Key (INT, DEFAULT 0), Facility_Key (INT), Descripcion (NVARCHAR(150))
   - Purpose: Maps BeFirst areas (PD_idt_areas) to PLEX operations (PART_OPERATION)

IMPORTANT: Read each file first before editing. Follow existing formatting. Only add minimal focused content. Do NOT restructure existing sections. If there's already a 2026-02-24 section in the changelog, add to it rather than creating a new one.

1→# BeFirst Platform Workflows
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→The BeFirst Manufacturing Intelligence Platform supports multiple workflow types across web applications, services, desktop applications, and ETL processes. This document describes the key workflows for each component.
     6→
     7→## Portal Workflows (BeFirst_Portal)
     8→
     9→### Authentication Workflow

    13→   ↓

    15→   ↓
    16→User enters credentials
    17→   ↓
    18→Azure AD validates and issues JWT token
    19→   ↓
    20→Portal receives token and establishes session
    21→   ↓
    22→User accesses Portal features based on Azure AD group membership
    23→```
    24→

   232→
   233→## Service Workflows (BeFirst_Services)
   234→
   235→### Background Service Patterns
   236→
   237→**40+ Windows Services** running continuously or on schedules, organized by function:
   238→
   239→#### Data Synchronization Services
   240→
   241→**PLEX to MES Sync:**
   242→```
   243→Timer triggers (every N minutes)
   244→   ↓
   245→Query PLEX for updated records
   246→   ↓
   247→Transform to MES schema

   252→```
   253→
   254→**Common sync services:**
   255→- Work order synchronization

   257→- Customer/Supplier sync

   260→
   261→#### Equipment Monitoring Services

   353→## Desktop Application Workflows
   354→
   355→### BeFirst_Production (Production Floor Monitoring)
   356→

   366→   ↓

   389→   ↓
   390→4. Drill into machine details
   391→   ↓
   392→5. Take corrective actions (adjust priorities, assign resources)
   393→```
   394→
   395→### frm_WAVE — Core Production Form
   396→
   397→<!-- Updated: 2026-02-24 -->
   398→
   399→`frm_WAVE` is the **primary operator form** for all intermediate manufacturing operations between SMT (Surface Mount Technology) and Packout. It handles the majority of production stations on the floor.
   400→
   401→**Scope — what frm_WAVE covers:**
   402→
   403→| Category | Examples |
   404→|---|---|
   405→| SMT post-processing | Wave solder, selective solder, conformal coating, cleaning |
   406→| Inspection | AOI, SPI, visual inspection, ICT (In-Circuit Test) |
   407→| Assembly | Manual assembly, press-fit, mechanical assembly |
   408→| Test | Functional test, burn-in, calibration |
   409→| Rework | AOI rework, repair stations |
   410→| Quality hold | Lock/release operations between production areas |
   411→
   412→**Scope — dedicated forms used instead of frm_WAVE:**
   413→
   414→| Operation | Form |
   415→|---|---|
   416→| Material receiving | `frm_Receive*` |
   417→| Shipping | `frm_a_b_*` shipping area |
   418→| Out-of-Box Audit (OOBA) | Dedicated OOBA form |
   419→| Label merging | MergeLabels form |
   420→| Unit merging | MergeUnits form |
   421→| Part number conversion | Conversion form |
   422→| Packout / boxing | `frm_PVS` |
   423→
   424→**Key behaviors common to all frm_WAVE stations:**
   425→
   426→- Operator selects part number (casilla) at start of shift — up to 6 simultaneous casillas
   427→- Serial numbers scanned or received via extractor (TCP, serial port, barcode scanner)
   428→- Each scan calls `enviarssn` → writes transaction to MES, optionally syncs to Plex ERP
   429→- Station-specific features activate based on DB configuration (locks, AOI review, pallet tracking, nozzle cleanliness, TCP server, etc.)
   430→- Downtime tracked automatically when no scan activity detected
   431→
   432→### frm_WAVE — TCP Server Integration
   433→
   434→<!-- Updated: 2026-02-24 -->
   435→
   436→Certain stations can receive serial and test result data directly from external equipment (vision systems, testers, in-circuit testers) over TCP instead of requiring manual barcode scanning.
   437→
   438→**Activation:** Configured per Part + Area + Side in the `PART_OPERATION_AREAS_TCP` table. When `CLIENT_OR_SERVER = 'SERVER'`, the form starts a TCP listener on load.
   439→
   440→**Message format received from equipment:**
   441→
   442→```
   443→PartNumber;SSN;Result;Ref1;Status1;Ref2;Status2;...
   444→
   445→Example: 90-00000-0000;ABC123456;False;A1;True;A2;False;B1;True
   446→```
   447→
   448→| Field | Example | Description |
   449→|---|---|---|
   450→| PartNumber | `90-00000-0000` | Must match the part loaded in the active casilla |
   451→| SSN | `ABC123456` | Unit serial number — passed to `enviarssn` |
   452→| Result | `True` / `False` | Overall pass/fail |
   453→| RefN;StatusN | `A2;False` | Circuit reference + individual result (repeating pairs) |
   454→
   455→**Processing flow:**
   456→
   457→```
   458→Equipment sends TCP message
   459→   ↓
   460→server_Handle_Client receives bytes on background thread
   461→   ↓
   462→Me.Invoke marshals to UI thread
   463→   ↓
   464→Parse: split by ";" → PartNumber, SSN, Result, circuit pairs
   465→   ↓
   466→Duplicate guard: skip if SSN = tcp_PreviousSerial_Scanned
   467→   ↓
   468→Part number validation:
   469→   ├─ Match → continue
   470→   └─ Mismatch → update casilla with new part, then continue
   471→   ↓
   472→If Result = Fail → collect failed circuit refs → build Comments string
   473→   ↓
   474→enviarssn(SSN, Now, AreaID, StationID, IsGood, ..., Comments)
   475→   ↓
   476→ACK "Message received." sent back to equipment
   477→```
   478→
   479→**Cleanup:** On form close (`Form1_FormClosing`), `tcp_isRunning = False` and `tcp_Listener.Stop()` are called in both exit branches.
   480→
   481→### frm_HGTech — Laser Serialization Skip
   482→
   483→<!-- Updated: 2026-02-24 -->
   484→
   485→`frm_HGTech` is the form for merging individual circuit boards into a panel master serial number (array/panel serialization via laser). Boards scanned here are linked under a parent SSN.
   486→
   487→**Laser Skip feature:** When a part number is configured in `PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_LASER_SKIP`, the `SkipLaserSerialization` flag is loaded as `True` during casilla selection. In `frm_HGTech`, all three TypeID merge paths (TypeID 1, 2, 3) include a skip guard before calling `publicar()`:
   488→
   489→```
   490→Unit scanned → duplicate/format checks → serialescazados/serialessinformato checks
   491→   ↓
   492→SkipLaserSerialization = True? → pub = False (skip merge)
   493→SkipLaserSerialization = False? → pub unchanged (proceed to merge)
   494→   ↓
   495→If pub Then publicar()  ← merge only executes when pub = True
   496→```
   497→
   498→**Configuration:**
   499→- Engineering Portal → Part Number Management → Serialization tab → Laser Serialization Skip section
   500→- Add the part number ID to `PART_OPERATION_SERIALIZATION_LASER_SKIP`
   501→- Takes effect at next casilla load (no restart required)
   502→
   503→**When to use:** Applicable when laser serialization is not required for a specific part number variant but the part still passes through the frm_HGTech station for other operations.
   504→
   505→### frm_Rework — Defect Recording and Unit Release
   506→
   507→<!-- Updated: 2026-02-24 -->
   508→
   509→`frm_Rework` is the form used to record rework activity on units marked as defective and to release them back into the production flow. Operators document which components failed, how they were repaired, and a QA supervisor approves or rejects the release.
   510→
   511→#### Three-Step Process (when `req_rework_threesteps = True`)
   512→
   513→The rework workflow moves through three dedicated stations configured in `ExData`:
   514→
   515→| Step | Area Config Key | Role | Who Uses It |
   516→|---|---|---|---|
   517→| 1 | `CONF_diagnostic_area_id` | **Diagnostics** — identify the root cause and failed components | Technician |
   518→| 2 | `CONF_rework_area_id` | **Repair** — record the actual repair, components replaced | Repair operator |
   519→| 3 | `CONF_rework_qa_area_id` | **QA** — review repaired unit, Accept or Reject release | QA inspector |
   520→
   521→When `req_rework_threesteps = False`, steps 1 and 2 are combined into a single operation.
   522→
   523→#### Unit Status Model
   524→
   525→The function `getBeFirstStatus(SSN)` queries `PD_tbl_units_wip` and returns one of these codes:
   526→
   527→| Return Value | Meaning |
   528→|---|---|
   529→| `-2` | SCRAP (`DefectCodeID = 92`) |
   530→| `-1` | Good unit — not in rework flow |
   531→| `0` | New / untracked unit |
   532→| `CONF_diagnostic_area_id` | Currently pending at Diagnostics |
   533→| `CONF_rework_area_id` | Currently pending at Repair |
   534→| `CONF_rework_qa_area_id` | Currently pending at QA |
   535→
   536→Each area validates the status before accepting the scan and routes the operator to the next allowed step.
   537→
   538→#### Operator Workflow
   539→
   540→```
   541→Operator scans SSN → getBeFirstStatus() validates current state
   542→   ↓
   543→Area = Diagnostics:
   544→  ├─ Status 0 or Diagnostics → proceed to select part number + defects
   545→  └─ Already in Repair/QA → warn operator (allow override)
   546→
   547→Area = Repair:
   548→  ├─ Status at Diagnostics → load prior defect info (BuscarDatosSN) → proceed
   549→  └─ Status 0 → treat as new rework
   550→
   551→Area = QA:
   552→  ├─ Status at Repair → load details (BuscarDatosSN) → ACCEPT or REJECT
   553→  └─ Others → block or warn
   554→   ↓
   555→Operator selects Part Number + records defects (drag circuit ref → defect code)
   556→   ↓
   557→(Optional) Multi-unit mode or No-Label mode
   558→   ↓
   559→Submit → InsertSN() writes tracking + WIP + history
   560→   ↓
   561→If QA ACCEPT: unit WIP restored to previous production area (Rework_Move_Previous_Area config)
   562→If Rework_MoveContainerToOK enabled: Plex container moved from INTERNAL DEFECTIVE to OK
   563→```
   564→
   565→#### Defect Recording
   566→
   567→Defects are captured by drag-and-drop:
   568→- Left panel: `dgv_CircuitRef` — circuit reference BOM from `RW_tbl_bom` for the selected part
   569→- Right panel: `dgv_Defects` — defect codes from `EN_idt_defects_codes` filtered by category
   570→- Drag a circuit row onto a defect row → appends `"ComponentRef - DefectCode,"` to `txt_Defects`
   571→- Components are stored in `ExData.Rework_dtComponentes` and flushed to `RW_tbl_components_used` on submit
   572→
   573→Guards before adding a defect:
   574→- `RW_tbl_bom_lock` — component/location locked for this part → blocked with message
   575→- `Quality_Inspection_Rework_Lock` — QA-locked circuit reference → blocked
   576→- `Part_Operation_Rework_ComponentConfirm` — material confirmation required → opens `frm_Rework_Material_Confirm`
   577→
   578→#### Release Guards (QA Step)
   579→
   580→Before releasing a unit at the QA station, two additional checks run:
   581→
   582→| Guard | Table | Behaviour |
   583→|---|---|---|
   584→| AOI required | `Part_Operation_Rework_Requires_AOI` | Block release — send unit to AOI first |
   585→| CFT approval required | `Part_Operation_Rework_RequiresCFT` | Opens `frm_a_d_unlockCFT` — CFT engineer must approve |
   586→
   587→#### 5-Failures Rule
   588→
   589→At every area, after recording the scan, the total rework count for that SSN at the current area is checked. If ≥ 5, a QA lock record is inserted into `QA_tbl_lockunitspvs` and the operator is notified.
   590→
   591→#### Inactive Part Number Handling
   592→
   593→`LoadPNs()` loads only `isactive=1` parts into `ddlPartNumber`. When a scanned SSN carries an inactive PartID (revision change), `getBeFirstStatus()` dynamically adds that part to the dropdown with an `[INACTIVE]` suffix so the operator can continue without error.
   594→
   595→#### Key Feature Flags
   596→
   597→| Flag | Source | Effect |
   598→|---|---|---|
   599→| `req_rework_threesteps` | `ExData` | Enables 3-step Diag/Repair/QA flow |
   600→| `req_rework_allows_goods` | `ExData` | Allows good units to pass through QA rework |
   601→| `CONF_Rework_Select_PN_AfterScan` | `ExData` | Auto-selects part number after SSN scan |
   602→| `Rework_MoveContainerToOK` | `CF_idt_defaults` | Moves Plex container to OK on QA accept |
   603→| `Rework_Move_Previous_Area` | `CF_idt_defaults` | Restores WIP to previous production route stage |
   604→
   605→#### Key Tables
   606→
   607→| Table | Purpose |
   608→|---|---|
   609→| `PD_tbl_units_wip` | Current defect status and routing |
   610→| `PD_tbl_units_tracking` | Permanent production history per scan |
   611→| `RW_tbl_bom` | Rework BOM — component references per part |

   663→### BeFirst_ICTInline (Keysight ICT Test Integration)
   664→
   665→**ICT Test Integration Workflow:**
   666→
   667→```
   668→Keysight i3070 Test Plan Execution
   669→   ↓
   670→1. Test plan reads operation code (1-6) to operacion.txt
   671→   ↓
   672→2. Test plan writes test data to input files:
   673→   - ssn.txt (Serial Number)
   674→   - datos_ICT.txt (Part number, station, area)
   675→   - Config.ini (Server URL)
   676→   ↓
   677→3. Test plan calls API_ICTv1.7.3.exe with operation code
   678→   ↓
   679→4. Executable authenticates with BeFirst API:
   680→   - GET /Account/GetTokenAsync
   681→   - Stores bearer token to Access_Token.txt
   682→   ↓
   683→5. Executable processes operation based on code:
   684→
   685→   Operation 1 - Get Part ID:
   686→   - GET /Parts/Get?PartNumber={partnumber}
   687→   - Returns PartID for part number
   688→   - Writes to datos.txt
   689→
   690→   Operation 2 - Get Station ID:
   691→   - GET /Station/Get?StationName={hostname}
   692→   - Returns StationID for test equipment
   693→   - Writes to datos.txt
   694→
   695→   Operation 3 - Get Child Units:
   696→   - GET /Units/Array/GetChilds?ChildOrParent={ssn}
   697→   - Returns child units from panel (array)
   698→   - Writes to hijas.txt
   699→
   700→   Operation 4 - Check In:
   701→   - POST /Units/CheckIn
   702→   - Params: PartID, SSN, AreaID, Side, StationID, TrnDate, NestID
   703→   - Registers unit/panel in manufacturing execution system
   704→   - Returns check-in status
   705→
   706→   Operation 5 - Post Setup:
   707→   - POST /Units/CheckIn (with setup flag)
   708→   - Pre-test setup validation
   709→
   710→   Operation 6 - Check Out:
   711→   - POST /Units/CheckOut (pass) or /Units/CheckOutParametrized (fail)
   712→   - Pass: Records successful test
   713→   - Fail: Records failure with detailed test parameters
   714→   - Params: PartID, SSN, Result, AreaID, Side, StationID, TrnDate
   715→   - Parametric data: Value, Min_Value, Max_Value, Nom_Value, TestStep
   716→   ↓
   717→6. Executable writes results to output files:
   718→   - Result.txt (Check-in/check-out status)
   719→   - datos.txt (Retrieved IDs)
   720→   - hijas.txt (Child unit list)
   721→   - program.log (Detailed logging)
   722→   ↓
   723→7. Test plan reads output files and continues test execution
   724→   ↓
   725→8. On test failure:
   726→   - Test plan writes failure details to datos_ICT.txt
   727→   - Calls executable with operation 6 (check-out with parameters)
   728→   - Executable posts detailed parametric data to API
   729→   ↓
   730→Test results recorded in TESTERS_RESULTS database
   731→```

   733→### Test Plan Template (`Testplan_con_API.txt`)
   734→
   735→**Purpose:** Reference implementation showing how to integrate Keysight i3070 test plans with BeFirst APIs via the `API_ICTv1.7.3.exe` executable.
   736→
   737→**Key Components:**
   738→
   739→1. **PLC Integration (Omron)**:
   740→   - Automated board loading/unloading
   741→   - Press control (all probes, long probes, retrieve positions)
   742→   - Barcode enable/disable (bypass modes)
   743→   - Tower light and buzzer control
   744→   - Bottom fixture lock/unlock
   745→
   746→2. **Panelization Support**:
   747→   - Handles multi-board panels (example: 4 boards per panel)
   748→   - Uses `BoardSet_boards_1_to_4(1:4)` array for panel testing
   749→   - Supports individual board serial number tracking within panels
   750→
   751→3. **BeFirst API Integration Points**:
   752→   - **Before Test**: Call operation 4 (Check-In) to register unit/panel
   753→   - **During Test**: Execute analog/digital tests per test plan
   754→   - **After Test**: Call operation 6 (Check-Out) with pass/fail and parametric data
   755→   - **Error Handling**: Retry logic for API communication failures
   756→
   757→4. **Barcode Workflow**:
   758→   - Wait for PLC signal (board ready for barcode scan)
   759→   - Scan board barcode or read from panel master serial
   760→   - Validate barcode against expected part number
   761→   - Enable bypass mode for manual testing or retest scenarios
   762→
   763→5. **Pass/Fail Routing**:
   764→   - **Pass**: PLC command 4101 (Board Out PASSED) → green tower light
   765→   - **Fail**: PLC command 4103 (Board Out FAILED) → red tower light, rework bin
   766→   - **Retest**: PLC command 4009 (Move Press to Retest Position)
   767→
   768→**Integration Template Structure:**
   769→
   770→```
   771→! Testplan for [Part Number]
   772→! Includes BeFirst API integration via API_ICTv1.7.3.exe
   773→
   774→! 1. PLC Communication Setup
   775→!    - Define PLC read/write addresses
   776→!    - Configure handler control commands
   777→
   778→! 2. Test Initialization
   779→!    - Initialize test variables
   780→!    - Set custom options (learn mode, data logging, etc.)
   781→!    - Configure barcode scanning
   782→
   783→! 3. Pre-Test BeFirst API Calls
   784→!    - Write operation code 4 to operacion.txt (Check-In)
   785→!    - Write SSN to ssn.txt
   786→!    - Write part/station/area to datos_ICT.txt
   787→!    - Call API_ICTv1.7.3.exe
   788→!    - Read Result.txt for check-in confirmation
   789→
   790→! 4. Test Execution
   791→!    - Run analog tests (power supply, continuity, etc.)
   792→!    - Run digital tests (boundary scan, functional tests)
   793→!    - Collect parametric data (voltages, resistances, timing)
   794→
   795→! 5. Post-Test BeFirst API Calls
   796→!    - Write operation code 6 to operacion.txt (Check-Out)
   797→!    - If PASS: Simple check-out (Result.txt)
   798→!    - If FAIL: Parametrized check-out with failure data
   799→!    - Read Result.txt for check-out confirmation
   800→
   801→! 6. PLC Result Routing
   802→!    - Send pass/fail command to PLC
   803→!    - PLC routes board to pass bin or rework bin
   804→!    - Update tower light and buzzer status
   805→```
   806→
   807→**Example BeFirst API Call in Test Plan:**
   808→
   809→```basic
   810→! Check-In Example (before test execution)
   811→! Write operation code 4 to operacion.txt
   812→! Write SSN to ssn.txt
   813→! Write part number, station, area to datos_ICT.txt
   814→! Call API executable
   815→! Read Result.txt for confirmation
   816→
   817→! Check-Out Example (after test execution, on failure)
   818→! Write operation code 6 to operacion.txt
   819→! Write failure details to datos_ICT.txt
   820→! Call API executable with parametric data
   821→! Read Result.txt for confirmation
   822→```
   823→
   824→**Customization for New Test Stations:**
   825→
   826→To adapt this template for a new ICT tester:
   827→
   828→1. **Update PLC Addresses**: Modify PLC read/write addresses for your handler configuration
   829→2. **Configure Part Numbers**: Update part number definitions and BOM references
   830→3. **Set API Endpoints**: Configure `Config.ini` with correct BeFirst API server URL
   831→4. **Adjust Panelization**: Modify board array dimensions (1:4, 1:2, etc.) for your panel size
   832→5. **Map Stations**: Ensure station name in test plan matches station configuration in BeFirst MES
   833→6. **Test API Communication**: Run check-in/check-out operations manually to verify connectivity
   834→
   835→**Location:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_ICTInline\Testplan_con_API.txt`
   836→
   837→**Supported Operations:**
   838→
   839→| Code | Operation | API Endpoint | Purpose |
   840→|------|-----------|--------------|---------|
   841→| 1 | GetPartID | /Parts/Get | Lookup part ID by part number |
   842→| 2 | GetStationID | /Station/Get | Lookup station ID by hostname |
   843→| 3 | Get Child Units | /Units/Array/GetChilds | Get panel child units (array) |
   844→| 4 | Check In | /Units/CheckIn | Start test session |
   845→| 5 | Post Setup | /Units/CheckIn (setup) | Pre-test setup validation |
   846→| 6 | Check Out | /Units/CheckOut or /Units/CheckOutParametrized | Record test results (pass/fail with parametrics) |
   847→
   848→**Data Flow:**
   849→
   850→```
   851→Keysight Test Plan → Text Files → API_ICTv1.7.3.exe → BeFirst API → TESTERS_RESULTS DB
   852→                                         ↓
   853→                                   program.log (troubleshooting)
   854→```

   856→**Error Handling:**
   857→
   858→- **401 Unauthorized**: Auto-refresh bearer token and retry operation
   859→- **200 with error**: Detect error responses in 200 OK responses, log to error files
   860→- **Network errors**: Retry with exponential backoff
   861→- **All errors logged to**:
   862→  - `program.log` - Detailed diagnostic logging
   863→  - `errores_checkin_YYYY-MM-DD.txt` - Daily check-in error log
   864→  - `errores_checkout_YYYY-MM-DD.txt` - Daily check-out error log
   865→
   866→**Async Performance Optimization:**
   867→
   868→- Uses `aiohttp` for non-blocking HTTP requests
   869→- Async check-in/check-out operations for multi-unit panels
   870→- Parallel API calls for child unit operations
   871→- Reduces test time overhead by 30-50%
   872→
   873→**Deployment:**
   874→
   875→- Executable deployed to test equipment workstations
   876→- No Python runtime required (PyInstaller standalone build)
   877→- Config.ini for server URL configuration (https://10.120.40.11:8443)
   878→- Text file-based interface for Keysight test plan integration

   880→**Multi-Facility Support:**
